briefdescription.para: Get a pointer to an instance of a SecondaryEvent if this connection supports secondary events.

detaileddescription.para: To prevent erronous calls of this function on primary connections, the base class implementation below just contains assert(false).

briefdescription.para: Get all properties of this connection and put them into a dictionary.
Set properties of this connection from the values given in dictionary.

simplesect.para: Target and Rport cannot be changed after a connection has been created.

briefdescription.para: Check syn_spec dictionary for parameters that are not allowed with the given connection.

detaileddescription.para: Will issue warning or throw error if an illegal parameter is found. The method does nothing if no illegal parameter is found.

simplesect.para: Classes requiring checks need to override the function with their own implementation, as this base class implementation does not do anything.

briefdescription.para: Calibrate the delay of this connection to the desired resolution.
Return the delay of the connection in ms.
Return the delay of the connection in steps.
Set the delay of the connection.
Set the delay of the connection in steps.
Set the synapse id of the connection.
Get the synapse id of the connection.

memberdef.argsstring: (const size_t, const std::vector< spikecounter > &, const double, const CommonSynapseProperties &)
memberdef.name: trigger_update_weight

briefdescription.para: Triggers an update of a synaptic weight.

detaileddescription.para: This function is needed for neuromodulated synaptic plasticity

briefdescription.para: Sets a flag in the connection to signal that the following connection has the same source.
Returns a flag denoting whether the connection has source subsequent targets.
Returns a flag denoting if the connection is disabled.
This function calls check_connection() on the sender to check if the receiver accepts the event type and receptor type requested by the sender.

parameterdescription.para: The ID of the requested receptor type
last spike produced by the presynaptic neuron (for STDP and maturing connections)

detaileddescription.para: It provides the mandatory properties receiver port and target, as well as the functions get_status() and set_status() to read and write them. A suitable Connector containing these connections can be obtained from the template GenericConnector.

simplesect.para: Please note that the event received by the send() function is a reference to a single object that is re-used by each Connection. This means that the object must not be changed in the Connection, or if needs to be changed, everything has to be reset after sending (i.e. after Event::operator() has been called).
